anchor.snippet: A triangle is a polygon with three corners and three sides

annotations.label: Sum of angles equals straight angle

anchor.snippet: the sum of angles of a triangle always equals a straight angle

annotations.label: Area equals half base times height

anchor.snippet: The area of a triangle equals one-half the product of height and base length

annotations.label: Three non-collinear points determine a triangle

anchor.snippet: any three points that do not all lie on the same straight line determine a unique triangle
A geodesic triangle is a region of a general two-dimensional surface enclosed by three sides
A curvilinear triangle is a shape with three curved sides
A triangle is a figure consisting of three line segments, each of whose endpoints are connected
A triangle whose sides are all the same length is an equilateral triangle
A triangle in which one of the angles is a right angle is a right triangle

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: A perpendicular bisector of a side of a triangle is a straight line passing through the midpoint of the side

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The three perpendicular bisectors meet in a single point, the triangle's circumcenter

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: Thales' theorem implies that if the circumcenter is located on the side of the triangle, then the angle opposite that side is a right angle

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: If the circumcenter is located inside the triangle, then the triangle is acute

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: An altitude of a triangle is a straight line through a vertex and perpendicular to the opposite side

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The three altitudes intersect in a single point, called the orthocenter of the triangle

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The orthocenter lies inside the triangle if and only if the triangle is acute

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: An angle bisector of a triangle is a straight line through a vertex that cuts the corresponding angle in half

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The three angle bisectors intersect in a single point, the incenter

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: There are three other important circles, the excircles ; they lie outside the triangle and touch one side

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The midpoints of the three sides and the feet of the three altitudes all lie on a single circle, the triangle's nine-point circle

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: all lie on a single line, known as Euler's line

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: A median of a triangle is a straight line through a vertex and the midpoint of the opposite side

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The three medians intersect in a single point, the triangle's centroid

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: The centroid cuts every median in the ratio 2:1

anchor.section: Points, lines, and circles associated with a triangle
anchor.snippet: If one reflects a median in the angle bisector that passes through the same vertex, one obtains a symmedian
The sum of the measures of the interior angles of a triangle in Euclidean space is always 180 degrees
The measure of an exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the measures of the two interior angles that are not adjacent to it
The sum of the measures of the three exterior angles (one for each vertex) of any triangle is 360 degrees
They can be defined as the ratio between any two sides of a right triangle
methods for doing so use the law of sines and the law of cosines

annotations.label: Three angles summing to 180° form a triangle

anchor.snippet: Any three angles that add to 180° can be the internal angles of a triangle
A degenerate triangle , whose vertices are collinear , has internal angles of 0° and 180°
Two triangles are said to be similar , if every angle of one triangle has the same measure as the corresponding angle in the other triangle
If and only if one pair of internal angles of two triangles have the same measure as each other, and another pair also have the same measure as each other, the triangles are similar

If and only if one pair of corresponding sides of two triangles are in the same proportion as another pair of corresponding sides, and their included angles have the same measure, then the triangles are similar
If and only if three pairs of corresponding sides of two triangles are all in the same proportion, then the triangles are similar
Two triangles that are congruent have exactly the same size and shape
SAS Postulate: Two sides in a triangle have the same length as two sides in the other triangle, and the included angles have the same measure
ASA: Two interior angles and the side between them in a triangle have the same measure and length
SSS: Each side of a triangle has the same length as the corresponding side of the other triangle
AAS: Two angles and a corresponding (non-included) side in a triangle have the same measure and length
